channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: France's re-appointed prime minister Lecornu calls for calm amid political chaos
item.description: L'HAY-LES-ROSES, France (AP) — France’s newly re-appointed prime minister acknowledged Saturday that there weren’t ″a lot of candidates″ for his job — and that he might not last long in the post given the country’s deep political divides.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel, amid Gaza ceasefire, kills at least 1 in overnight strikes on Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military launched airstrikes on Lebanon overnight targeting Hezbollah and killing at least one person. The attack on the village of Msayleh also destroyed a highway that connects Lebanon to Beirut, the Lebanese Health Ministry said, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Palestinians return to ruins as aid groups push to scale up deliveries and US troops land in Israel
item.description: Tens of thousands of displaced Palestinians are returning to their neighborhoods as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas enters its second day.
item.dc:creator: Abdel Kareem Hana, Sam Metz, Sarah El Deeb And Sam Mednick, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Korea displays new long-range missile at a military parade celebrating Kim Jong Un
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — At a massive military parade attended by foreign leaders, North Korean leader Kim Jong Un rolled out his nuclear-armed military’s most powerful weapons, including a new intercontinental ballistic missile he may be preparing to test in coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Power restored to 800,000 in Kyiv after major Russian strikes on Ukraine's energy grid
item.description: Power has been restored to over 800,000 residents in Kyiv, a day after Russia launched major attacks on Ukraine's power grid, causing widespread blackouts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel rejects freeing from prison the most popular Palestinian leader
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — The most popular and potentially unifying Palestinian leader — Marwan Barghouti — is not among the prisoners Israel intends to free in exchange for hostages held by Hamas under the new Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH, JULIA FRANKEL and JALAL BWAITAL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Thousands of Palestinians return to what's left of their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians headed back to the heavily destroyed northern Gaza Strip on Friday as a U.S.-brokered ceasefire came into effect in a deal that raised hopes for ending the Israel-Hamas war. All the remaining hostages were set to be released within days.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago leaders, residents share mixed emotions and cautious hope after Israel-Hamas ceasefire announcement
item.description: Israel and Hamas have agreed to a pause in their devastating two-year war and the release of the remaining hostages in exchange for Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Angelica Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza return north as ceasefire takes effect
item.description: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza were starting to walk north to return to their homes Friday, after the Israeli military said the ceasefire agreement with Hamas came into effect at noon local time and that its soldiers were withdrawing to the agreed-upon deployment lines.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin praises Trump's peace efforts as 'really doing a lot' to resolve global crises and conflicts
item.description: President Donald Trump's efforts to negotiate peace deals, including the Israel-Hamas truce, received praise from Russian President Vladimir Putin at a summit in Tajikistan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces 100 percent tariff on China in response to rare earth controls
item.description: President Trump announced Friday he will raise tariffs on China in response to a move from Beijing to tighten its control over certain critical minerals and rare earth elements. Trump announced on Truth Social that he would impose a tariff of 100 percent on Chinese goods beginning Nov. 1 or sooner.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize bets on Polymarket raise leak concerns, prompts probe
item.description: The last-minute bets, first reported by local outlets, are a cause for concern for the Norwegian officials in charge of awarding the honor
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Dutch far-right politician Wilders fears he may have been a target of Belgian attack plotters
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Dutch far-right politician Geert Wilders said Friday that he’s suspending campaigning for national elections later this month following reports that he was a possible target of a suspected plot in Belgium to kill politicians using a drone carrying explosives.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With ceasefire in effect, thousands trek to northern Gaza
item.description: As thousands of displaced Palestinians celebrated the ceasefire, Gaza-based photojournalist Fadel Mghari captured images of their trek home.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, The Hill

item.title: Nobel winner dedicates peace prize to people of Venezuela, Trump
item.description: This year's Nobel Peace Prize winner, Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado, has dedicated her award to the people of her country and to President Trump for his "decisive support” for democracy in Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Surina Venkat, The Hill

item.title: PHOTOS: With ceasefire in effect, thousands trek to northern Gaza
item.description: As thousands of displaced Palestinians celebrated the ceasefire, Gaza-based photojournalist Fadel Mghari captured images of their trek home.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Melania Trump in talks with Putin on reuniting Ukrainian children with families
item.description: First lady Melania Trump said Friday she has been in communication with Russian President Vladimir Putin about reuniting Ukrainian children displaced by war with their families.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Trump threatens countermeasures after China tightens grip on rare earths, says 'no reason' to meet Xi
item.description: President Trump on Friday threatened an increase in tariffs on China and other potential countermeasures after Beijing tightened control over rare earth elements and critical mineral exports.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Israel-Hamas ceasefire takes effect, IDF begins pulling back in Gaza
item.description: Israeli troops began repositioning to pre-determined locations as the ceasefire with Hamas began, bringing the hostages one step closer to going home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Opposition activist María Corina Machado of Venezuela wins the Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: The former opposition presidential candidate in Venezuela was lauded for being a “key, unifying figure in a political opposition that was once deeply divided — an opposition that found common ground in the demand for free elections and representative government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's quest for the Nobel Peace Prize falls short again despite high-profile nominations
item.description: The White House is responding bitterly to President Donald Trump being passed over for the Nobel Peace Prize despite efforts from Republicans and world leaders.
item.dc:creator: Mike Pesoli, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: King Mohammed VI set to speak as Gen Z protesters demand reforms in Morocco
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco's elusive King Mohammed VI inaugurates parliament Friday in a speech with far higher stakes than in recent years, as anti-government protests sweep the nation's cities, questioning the government's spending priorities.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The West Bank's dwindling Palestinian Christian communities continue to struggle amid violence
item.description: TAYBEH, West Bank (AP) — Early on Sundays, bells call the faithful to worship at the three churches in this hilltop village that the Gospel narrates Jesus visited. It is now the last entirely Christian one in the occupied West Bank.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Tropical Storm Jerry churns in the Atlantic, Priscilla and a nor'easter threaten US with flooding
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Tropical Storm Jerry churned Thursday in the Atlantic on its approach to the Leeward Islands as Tropical Storm Priscilla and Tropical Storm Raymond moved along Mexico’s Pacific coast, threatening heavy rain and flooding in their paths, forecasters said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Exclusive: Israeli ambassador says no peace in Gaza unless Hamas hands over all 48 hostages, disarms
item.description: Israel's Ambassador Yechiel Leiter warns the Gaza war will continue unless Hamas returns all hostages and completely disarms under the new peace agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump peace deal triggers 72-hour countdown for Hamas to release 48 hostages from Gaza
item.description: Israeli security cabinet will convene Thursday to approve the Hamas hostage deal, starting a 96-hour countdown for the release of 48 hostages held for over two years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pushing for drastic reduction in refugees allowed into US
item.description: "Our concern is that this could turn what has long been a globally responsive humanitarian system into one that overwhelmingly favors a single group.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Before and after images show devastating destruction in Gaza
item.description: Satellite imagery reveals widespread devastation in Gaza cities, including Rafah and Beit Hanoun, since the start of the Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian strike seriously damages Ukrainian power plant as winter approaches, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia seriously damaged one of Ukraine’s thermal power plants in an overnight attack, authorities said Wednesday, as Moscow pursued its campaign to deny Ukrainians heat, light and running water as winter approaches.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Cuban-led caravan aims for Mexico City as Trump policies deter migrants from US
item.description: Cuban migrants in Mexico choose Mexico City over U.S. destinations as President Donald Trump's strict immigration policies influence regional migration trends.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Snowstorm traps hundreds of hikers on Mount Everest during China's national holiday
item.description: About 350 hikers had reached a meeting point in Tingri country and rescuers were in contact with another 200, state broadcaster CCTV said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Israeli and Hamas officials discuss US peace plan for Gaza
item.description: Israeli and Hamas officials are holding indirect talks in an Egyptian resort Monday on a U.S. peace plan for Gaza.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rescuers in India search for missing after landslides kill 24 in Darjeeling region
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Rescuers were scouring India's northeastern tea-growing hill district of Darjeeling on Monday for those reported missing a day after landslides triggered by heavy downpours killed at least 24 people, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: French Prime Minister resigns after less than a month in office
item.description: Facing criticism from all sides, France’s new prime minister Sébastien Lecornu resigned less than 24 hours after naming his government
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Japan’s ruling party elects Sanae Takaichi as new leader, likely to become first female PM
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan’s governing party on Saturday elected former Economic Security Minister Sanae Takaichi, a hard-line ultra-conservative and China hawk, as its new leader, making her likely to become the country’s first female prime minister.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, WGN 9
